stories.title: Iran's strike hurt up to 20 U.S. personnel and damaged 2 radar jets in Saudi Arabia
stories.description: NPR has confirmed that two E-3 Sentry aircraft were damaged and as many as 20 U.S. service members were injured in an Iranian missile and drone attack on an air base in Saudi Arabia Friday.

stories.title: The rippling effect of the Iran war, seen from the border with Turkey
stories.description: U.S. marines have arrived in the Middle East. NPR's Emily Feng has been monitoring the latest developments from the border of Iran and Turkey.

stories.title: Iran warns U.S. against ground invasion, as Pakistan holds diplomatic talks
stories.description: A high-ranking Iranian official has accused the U.S. of planning a ground invasion as part of the next stage in the Iran war, and said such an intervention would be met with force.

stories.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels enter the fighting in the Middle East
stories.description: At least 15 U.S. military personnel were wounded in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the war with an attack on Israel.

stories.title: How the US could try to seize Iran's Kharg Island
stories.description: Donald Trump has indicated he could send troops to take control of Iran's key oil export terminal.

stories.title: Latin Patriarch will have access to Jerusalem holy site after police stopped entry
stories.description: The Israeli PM says Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa had been asked not to enter the church over concerns about Iranian strikes.

stories.title: Two Indonesian UN peacekeepers killed in explosion in Lebanon
stories.description: It is the second fatal incident in the last 24 hours, Unifil says, after Israel expanded its operation against Hezbollah.

stories.title: Potential Houthi threat to Red Sea shipping could further damage global economy
stories.description: The Iran-backed group could bring a second crucial waterway to a standstill, writes Sebastian Usher.

stories.title: Three Lebanese journalists killed in Israeli strike, say broadcasters
stories.description: The Israeli military confirms it killed Ali Shoeib and Mohamed Ftouni, alleging they also belonged to Hezbollah's military wing.

stories.title: Iran-backed Houthis join war with attack against Israel
stories.description: There are fears the Iranian proxy may attack shipping in the Red Sea, adding to global economic woes.

stories.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
stories.description: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.

stories.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
stories.description: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

stories.title: Ukraine signs deal with Saudi Arabia offering drone expertise
stories.description: Volodymyr Zelensky says the Gulf kingdom faces the same type of attacks from Iran that Kyiv has been resisting.

stories.title: Gaza peace doubts deepen as attention shifts to Iran
stories.description: With the world focused on the Iran war, there is increasing uncertainty about what happens next for Gaza.

stories.title: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
stories.description: One month since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, Tehran residents tell the BBC their lives have been devastated.

stories.title: Is Trump's pause on attacking Iranian energy for diplomacy or an escalation?
stories.description: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

stories.title: How the Hormuz closure could affect food, medicines and smartphones
stories.description: The price of a wide range of goods - from food, to smartphones, to medicines - could be affected by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: Fake images falsely claim Iran captured US marines on Kharg Island
'The Houthis hold serious cards' expert says, amid threats to choke off Red Sea passage
stories.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Dr Elisabeth Kendall, President of Girton College at the University of Cambridge, says that with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, the Red Sea has become a vital export route and that "if the Houthis choke off the vital exit point of Bab el...

stories.title: Trump's options in Iran range from bad to worse
stories.description: Reportedly frustrated that his war in Iran is dragging on longer than intended, US President Donald Trump has been conveying the message that it will be over soon. But with the Americans' ceasefire proposal leading nowhere, Washington's options now range from bad to worse. FRANCE...

stories.title: Sir Westmacott challenges Trump rationale as war on Iran sparks 'serious' economic fallout worldwide
stories.description: For Spotlight, François Picard is pleased to welcome Sir Peter Westmacott, distinguished ambassadorial fellow with the Atlantic Council's Europe Center and former British Ambassador to Turkey, France and the United States. As a senior British diplomat with decades of experience a...

stories.title: Boots on the ground? Trump eyes Iran oil hub Kharg Island
stories.description: What could possibly go wrong? As Donald Trump repeats threats of a Venezuela-style seizing of Tehran’s oil exports, as US assault troops get in position, the price of oil blowing past 110 dollars a barrel, and as markets take it at face value that the Pentagon's planning to make ...

stories.title: Three UN peacekeepers killed in south Lebanon in 24 hours amid Israel-Hezbollah conflict
stories.description: The UN said on Monday that two members of its peacekeeping force were killed in an explosion in south Lebanon, with a third being killed in a separate blast the previous day. Here's what to know about how the Blue Helmets are involved in the current conflict between Israel and He...

stories.title: Bab al-Mandab strait: Iran’s Houthi allies enter the conflict, raising fears over key trade route
stories.description: As Iran’s Houthi allies enter the conflict, Iran has threatened to disrupt shipping in the Bab al-Mandab Strait – a strategic waterway linking the Gulf of Aden to the Red Sea and the Suez Canal – with the help of its Houthi allies in Yemen. While disruptions in the Strait of Horm...

stories.title: While Trump vows Iran deal on the way, 'evidence points to another direction'
stories.description: US President Donald Trump threatened on March 30 to destroy Iran's crude export hub of Kharg Island, along with its oil wells and power plants, unless Tehran quickly accepted a peace deal. He also claimed that the United States was in 'serious discussions' with 'a more reasonable...

stories.title: France hands captain of suspected Russian 'shadow fleet' tanker one-year jail sentence
stories.description: A French court on Monday sentenced in absentia the captain of a suspected Russian shadow fleet tanker to one year in jail for failing to comply with orders to stop his ship. The vessel, which the French navy boarded in September before releasing the vessel, was thought to be tran...

stories.title: Ukraine signs defence agreements with Gulf Nations eager to fend off Iranian drones
stories.description: The Ukrainian president has hailed "historic" defence agreements with several Middle Eastern countries after his visit to the region last week. Volodymyr Zelensky has been seeking to capitalise on his military's drone-fighting prowess as countries like the United Arab Emirates an...

stories.title: Zelensky hails 'historic' defence agreements with Gulf states
stories.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Monday said he signed "historic" agreements with countries in the Middle East last week, including sharing Ukrainian expertise on downing drones as Iranian strikes continue to target Gulf countries. Ukraine has previously proposed an exch...

stories.title: US not 'postured' for another Iraq 2003: Iran maintains vast, 'high-quality military capability'
stories.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Michael Knights, Adjunct Fellow at The Washington Institute and Head of Research at Horizon Engage. He examines the evolving military dynamics between the US and Iran, focusing on the operational possibilities available to US forces and the stru...

stories.title: ‘We have to destroy Iran’s ability to launch missiles’, Rubio says
stories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the key to regional stability is eliminating Iran’s ability to launch missiles.

stories.title: Rubio says NATO must be re-examined after Spain blocks US operation on Iran
stories.description: Marco Rubio said it was “very disappointing” that NATO allies blocked the use of their bases for the US's war on Iran.

stories.title: French police make two more arrests over foiled attack on Bank of America
stories.description: Prosecutors investigating suspected link to Iran war due to similarities with other recent attempted attacks in Europe.

stories.title: ‘Strait of Hormuz will reopen one way or another’, Rubio tells Al Jazeera
stories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio has told Al Jazeera that the US will not accept Iran’s claim over the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Fire contained after missile attack on oil refinery in Israel’s Haifa
stories.description: Attack marks second time Oil Refineries facility in northern Israel targeted during US-Israeli war on Iran.

stories.title: Egypt’s president urges Trump to stop war on Iran
stories.description: Egypt’s President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi has called on Trump to end the war on Iran.

stories.title: Trump threatens to ‘blow up’ desalination plants in Iran if no deal reached
stories.description: Legal expert says targeting civilian sites would be 'collective punishment', which is prohibited under laws of war.

stories.title: Is the US ready to invade Iran? What Trump’s moves tell us so far
stories.description: Seizing strategic islands and destroying Iran's stock of enriched uranium are options the US is considering.

stories.title: ‘Loyalty campaign’: Iraqi armed groups in Iran as US talks of ground war
stories.description: Fighters from a major Iraqi pro-Iran armed group have entered the country, carrying aid — and a signal of support.

stories.title: Marco Rubio says President Trump prefers diplomatic solution to war on Iran
stories.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that President Donald Trump still prefers a diplomatic outcome for war on Iran.

stories.title: Israel passes law making death penalty default sentence for Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks
stories.description: Israel’s parliament approved a bill on Monday that would allow the execution of Palestinians convicted on terror charges for deadly attacks, a move that has been criticised as discriminatory and immediately drew a court challenge. Sixty-two lawmakers, including Prime Minister Ben...

stories.title: ‘All roads lead to higher prices and slower growth’: IMF says Iran war is dimming outlook for many economies
stories.description: The war in the Middle East has caused serious disruption to the economies of frontline countries, and is dimming the outlook for many economies that had just started to recover from previous crises, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) warned on Monday. In a blog published by th...

stories.title: Dar hints at US, Iran talks as quad pushes peace off-ramp
stories.description: • Saudi Arabia, Turkiye and Egypt back efforts to create conditions for Washington-Tehran structured negotiations• FMs explore ways for permanent end to hostilities, insist war is ‘not in favour of anyone’• Deputy PM to leave for Beijing to meet Chinese foreign minister tomorrow ...

stories.title: Washington hopes to reach deal with Tehran by April 6 — White House
stories.description: The US President had previously stated that Washington would destroy all of Iran’s power plants, oil wells, and the island of Kharg in the Gulf if Tehran were to derail talks with the US aimed at resolving the conflict

stories.title: Strikes on Iran may lead to nuclear arms race in Middle East, Russian senator warns
stories.description: Deputy chair of the Russian Federation Council Natalya Nikonorova noted that the US and Israel are pushing Iran towards pulling out of the NPT, "after which the country will be able to develop its nuclear program without control from the IAEA"

stories.title: US-Iran talks continue, going well — White House
stories.description: Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt noted that the US military continues to carry out strikes against Iran

stories.title: Iranian air defenses down US drone MQ-9 Reaper over Isfahan — IRGC
stories.description: The IRGC emphasized that the drone "was destroyed by an advanced air defense system, which is part of the country’s integrated air defenses."

stories.title: US troop buildup near Iran not sign of any new decisions — White House
stories.description: The United States and Israel launched a military operation against Iran on February 28

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz closure threatens to impact global fertilizer market — Rystad Energy
stories.description: According to the report, about 15% of global ammonia sales and 21% of urea, the most concentrated nitrogen fertilizer, are tied to exporters potentially impacted by the closure

stories.title: US interested in calling on Arab nations to help pay for cost of Iran operation — official
stories.description: The United States and Israel launched a military operation against Iran on February 28

stories.title: Iranian parliament committee approves Strait of Hormuz toll plan
stories.description: According to the plan, transiting the strait is banned for the ships associated with the US, Israel and the countries that have imposed unilateral sanctions on Iran

stories.title: Iran strikes US, Israeli military positions in region — IRGC
stories.description: According to the report, the targets were located in Bahrain, the United Arab Emirates, and Saudi Arabia

stories.title: Energy transit halt via Strait of Hormuz leads to largest oil market disruption — IMF
stories.description: The analysts said that for fuel-importing economies, the effect is that of a large, sudden tax on income

stories.title: Over 6,000 people hospitalized in Israel since operation against Iran — health ministry
stories.description: The department added that one of the injured is in critical condition, 16 are in serious condition

stories.title: Israel attacks 170 targets in Iran in 24 hours, using 400 munitions — IDF
stories.description: According to the Israel Defense Forces, the attacks targeted "a central site used to produce key components for a variety of weapons in Iran"

stories.title: Hezbollah takes responsibility for attack on Israeli military intelligence headquarters
stories.description: Missile strikes also targeted an Israeli naval base in Haifa Bay

stories.title: Four Seas Project could become basis for US plans in Persian Gulf — newspaper
stories.description: The initiative envisages the development of infrastructure and transport corridors linking the Black, Marmara, Aegean and Mediterranean Seas

stories.title: Iran considers Ukraine party to war against it — envoy to UN
stories.description: Amir Saeid Iravani noted that sending "hundreds of specialists" to the region "indicates active participation and facilitation of the unlawful use of force against a sovereign state"

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
